title: veronica serpyllifolia var. serpyllifolia
intro: Veronica serpyllifolia var. serpyllifolia, also known as thyme-leaved speedwell, is a low-growing, flowering plant in the Veronica genus, native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of thyme-leaved speedwell include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Thyme-leaved speedwell can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
